segments.text: "David went to the town of Nob to see Ahimelech the priest. Ahimelech was trembling in fear when he met him, and he asked,
Why are you here alone? Why isn't there anybody with you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The king has given me an assignment,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David replied.
He told me ‘Nobody must know anything about the assignment I have sent you to do.’ As for my men, I've told them where to meet me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: So what do you have on hand to eat? Let me have five loaves of bread, or whatever you can find.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: There's no ordinary bread,
segments.speaker: The priest

segments.text: the priest told David,
but there's some holy bread, as long as your men have not slept with any women lately.
segments.speaker: The priest

segments.text: We have not slept with any women,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David answered.
In fact that's the rule when I lead the troops on mission. They keep themselves pure even during ordinary missions, and all the more so right now.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "So the priest gave him the holy bread as they didn't have any other bread there except this
Bread of the Presence,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: which had been removed from the presence of the Lord that day and replaced with fresh bread.
"One of Saul's servants happened to be there that day, trying to make himself right with the Lord. He was Doeg the Edomite, Saul's chief shepherd.
Have you got a spear or sword here?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David asked Ahimelech.
I didn't bring my sword or any of my weapons with me, because what the king needed me to do was urgent.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "The priest replied,
The sword of Goliath the Philistine is here—the one you killed in the Valley of Elah. It's wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod. You can take it if you want. It's the only one here.
segments.speaker: The priest

segments.text: It's better than any other sword! Please give it to me,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David replied.
"David ran away from Saul that day and went to Achish, king of Gath.
"But Achish's officials asked the king,
Isn't this David, the king of that country? Didn't they sing about him in their dances, ‘Saul has killed his thousands, and David his tens of thousands’?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "David listened carefully to what they said and this made him very afraid of Achish, the king of Gath.
"So he changed the way he acted toward them and pretended to be crazy. He scratched marks on the town gates and let his spit run down his beard.
"Achish told his officials,
Look, you can see the man is completely mad! Why did you bring him to me?
segments.speaker: Achish

segments.text: Is it because I need more mad people that you've brought this man to go crazy right in front of me? Do you think I'm going to let him come into my house?
segments.speaker: Achish
